predictions.text: Large tech companies unprepared for disruption from agile AI-powered startups
WME and entertainment assets will continue consolidating value
AI will create new categories and opportunities in tech

dynamics: Sam delivers essay-style breakdown of three 'all-in individuals' (Egan Durban, Ari Emanuel, Dana White). Format is narrative analysis rather than interview. Shaan provides minimal interjections.
frameworks: All-in individuals framework - complete commitment to single bets
Hits singles and doubles, then steals third - compounding wins strategy
FU Money vs FU Loyalty, FU Happiness - alternative forms of security
Pre-fall vs Post-fall - life experience shapes resilience
Mail room as opportunity - unglamorous entry point filters commitment
learnings: Most successful people commit fully to concentrated bets rather than diversifying
Loyalty and partnership among elite operators creates moats and network effects
Adversity early in life builds psychological resilience that pays dividends
Being 'all-in' requires willingness to lose everything on conviction
Operating in unfashionable industries avoids competition with equally talented peers

banger_quotes.quote: The key to success is dumb competition
You can always come back and be a bellhop, but you can't get back this time
You need to know when you're on the field to compete and win
My goal is to find one or two bets per year that I can't say no to

numbers_dropped: Silver Lake: $72 billion in assets under management
Skype deal: bought for $1.9B, sold to Microsoft for $8.5B
Alibaba investment: 6x return on initial investment at $35B market cap
Manchester City: Silver Lake owns $500M stake
UFC: sold for $4 billion (2000x return for original $2M investment)
Fertitta brothers owned ~40% each when UFC sold
notable: Essay format analyzing successful entrepreneurs' common traits; focus on commitment and willingness to concentrate rather than diversify
